title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: This cycle’s strongest signal is operational: AI safety work is increasingly about deployment controls, external review, and governance mechanisms around real-world use. The fresh updates span coding-agent containment, mental-health intervention features, independent scrutiny of frontier risk claims, and implementation details for transparency rules in the EU.

items.title: Running Codex safely at OpenAI
items.why_it_matters: Shows concrete safeguards for coding agents, including sandboxing, approvals, and telemetry for higher-risk enterprise use.

items.title: METR reviews Anthropic’s automated R&D risk analysis
items.why_it_matters: Independent review sharpens how frontier labs substantiate claims about dangerous capability and misuse risk.

items.title: OpenAI expands Trusted Access for Cyber with GPT-5.5 models
items.why_it_matters: Access-gating for cyber models reflects a governance approach to dual-use capability deployment.

items.title: OpenAI introduces Trusted Contact in ChatGPT
items.why_it_matters: Adds an escalation mechanism for severe self-harm risk, extending safety beyond in-chat interventions.

items.title: Partnership on AI pushes AI-media transparency in EU Code of Practice
items.why_it_matters: Influences how disclosure and provenance rules for synthetic media may be operationalized in Europe.

items.title: OpenAI outlines how enterprises scale AI with trust and governance
items.why_it_matters: Signals that governance and quality controls are becoming core requirements for enterprise AI deployment.

items.title: OpenAI tests ads in ChatGPT with privacy and labeling commitments
items.why_it_matters: Ad integration raises new questions about answer integrity, user control, and privacy safeguards.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
